title: Fixed #35732 -- Wrapped Postgresql Concat args in parenthesis to fix operator precedence bug.

issues.body:
```suggestion
        search_term = "im matthew"
        self.assertSequenceEqual(
            self.Model.objects.annotate(
                concat_result=Concat(Value("I'm "), F("field")),
```

Does this add a `(...)::text` to the mix? it was missing that output_field that the bug happened but if the result of having output_field implies the existence of that cast, then the bug does not happen
@charettes please advise there, not sure what would be the most accurate reproduction of the bug we think we found
How about setting `output_field` on `Value` instead of on `Concat`?

  ``Concat`` is used in combination with ``trigram_similar`` due to using

issues.body:
```suggestion
* Fixed a regression in Django 5.1 that caused a crash when using the
  PostgreSQL lookup :lookup:`trigram_similar` on output fields from ``Concat``
  (:ticket:`35732`).
```
Maybe?
The release note should just detail what got fixed rather than how it was fixed :+1:
